extraction.one_line_summary: PAULS Wout won from the breakaway with 48km attack on final climbs while POGACAR attacked multiple times in final kilometer but VINGEGAARD responded easily
tactical_patterns: UAE Emirates isolated VINGEGAARD by driving tempo, but POGACAR's final-kilometer attacks yielded zero time despite YATES Adam support presence.
SOLER Marc's 48km attack triggered the winning move, suggesting UAE aggressive race-shaping even when POGACAR isn't directly benefiting.
RODRIGUEZ Carlos maintained GC podium position through consecutive Alpine stages, revealing unexpected stage-race resilience beyond time trial strength.

rider_signals.PAULS Wout: positive: First Grand Tour stage win at 35 after Monument victory, suggesting untapped stage-winning capability when freed from domestique duties
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: negative: Multiple final-kilometer attacks gained zero seconds on VINGEGAARD, signaling either declining superiority or opponent's improved form
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Responded comfortably to every POGACAR acceleration in final kilometer, showing defensive confidence lacking in previous Tour editions
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Dropped RODRIGUEZ on final climb while pacing teammate, demonstrating GC climbing legs stronger than fourth-place suggests
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: negative: Lost GC place to YATES despite defending champion status, revealing vulnerability in consecutive high-mountain stages
